title: Everything Everywhere All at Once
review: A maximalist masterpiece that somehow makes nihilism feel like the most hopeful thing in the universe.

title: Succession
review: The most precise portrait of power, family, and self-delusion ever put to screen.

title: Arrival
review: A film about language, time, and grief that completely reframes how you experience the narrative on rewatch.

title: The Bear
review: The most viscerally stressful television I've ever watched. Also the most human.

title: Oppenheimer
review: Nolan at the height of his powers. The Trinity test sequence is pure cinema.

title: Severance
review: A metaphor for compartmentalization that's also genuinely terrifying. One of the best pilots ever made.

title: Past Lives
review: The most devastating quiet film. Proves you don't need plot to break someone's heart.
